description: UE5 async: AsyncTask, FRunnable, ParallelFor, GameThread safety, and async asset loading

entries.title: Async Tasks & Game Thread Safety
entries.description: UE5 is primarily single-threaded for gameplay (Game Thread). Async operations run heavy work on background threads, but modifying UObjects, actors, or components must happen on the Game Thread.
entries.whatItIs: UE5's async system lets you offload expensive operations (loading, computation, HTTP requests) to background threads while keeping the Game Thread free for responsive gameplay. AsyncTask() and Async() schedule work on thread pools, and AsyncTask(ENamedThreads::GameThread, ...) marshals results back safely.
whenToUse: Loading large assets without blocking gameplay — async asset loading via FStreamableManager
Heavy computation that would cause frame hitches — pathfinding, AI decisions, procedural generation
Network/HTTP requests that should not block the Game Thread
File I/O (save/load) that takes more than a few milliseconds
ParallelFor: processing large arrays where each element is independent
whenNotToUse: For anything that modifies UObjects directly — must be done on the Game Thread
When the operation takes less than 1ms — the overhead of scheduling an async task is not worth it
When ordering matters and tasks depend on each other — use Futures or task chains, not fire-and-forget
For tick-based logic — just use Tick, which runs on the Game Thread already
commonMistakes: Modifying actors or components from a background thread — causes crashes or data corruption. Always marshal back to Game Thread.
Not checking IsInGameThread() before touching UObjects — silent corruption that crashes later
Creating async tasks that capture 'this' without checking if the object is still valid when the task completes
Using ParallelFor on a TArray while another thread is modifying it — no built-in thread safety on containers
Spawning too many async tasks without throttling — overwhelms the thread pool and causes worse performance than single-threaded
keyPoints: AsyncTask(ENamedThreads::AnyBackgroundThreadNormalTask, [](){ }): run work on a background thread
AsyncTask(ENamedThreads::GameThread, [](){ }): run work on the Game Thread (from a background thread)
Async(EAsyncExecution::ThreadPool, [](){ }): similar to AsyncTask, returns a TFuture<T>
ParallelFor(Count, [](int32 Index){ }): parallel loop over indices. Great for processing large arrays.
FStreamableManager: async asset loading. RequestAsyncLoad() with completion callback.
IsInGameThread(): check if current code is running on the Game Thread
Rule: READ from UObjects on any thread (carefully), WRITE only on Game Thread
